---
title: "Digital Land: Who Captures the Value When the Cloud Touches the Ground?"
format:
  pdf:
    fontsize: 12pt
    linestretch: 2
    geometry: margin=1in
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

<!--
HGSSS 2026 writing contest entry — Theme: Land Value Taxation.
ANONYMIZED: no author name, school, or identifying info anywhere in this file
(rules: identity goes in the submission email only — refs/rules.md).
Length: 10–25 pages double-spaced. Compressed from paper/paper.qmd on essay day
(plan: Day 9). Structure mirrors the paper minus the methods appendix:

1. Cold open — the $26.1M land / $1.81B servers split (sources.md S4)
2. George's savannah, compressed (S10 quote, checked against Gutenberg)
3. Georgia's boom in numbers (S1, S3, S5)
4. What announcements do to home values — headline result + event-study figure
   (every number interpolated from ../output/results.json)
5. The value that may never arrive — fan chart (results.json monte_carlo)
6. Land value capture as the Georgist remedy (OECD/ITF; Plassmann & Tideman)
7. Honest limitations paragraph (ARCHITECTURE §7)

DO NOT hand-type any statistic. Same interpolation pattern as paper.qmd.
-->

```{python}
#| label: load-results
import json
from pathlib import Path
res_path = Path("../output/results.json")
res = json.loads(res_path.read_text()) if res_path.exists() else {}
```

*(Essay body drafted on essay day from the finished paper — this stub exists so
`quarto render essay/essay.qmd` is wired up and anonymization is checked from
day one.)*
